steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that best describes the overall structure of the passage. Look for two key moves the passage makes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The text mentions a collection of essays and then points out something about these essays that is not completely known.: The passage introduces The Federalist Papers, then shifts with "Though" to reveal that some authorship is in question. Two-part structure matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — The text summarizes an argument made in a collection of essays and then suggests that the essays' authors didn't unanimously agree with the argument.: The passage raises uncertainty about authorship, not about the arguments made. ✗
  • Choice C — The text lists the authors of a collection of essays and then notes that some of the essays were written by one person, while others were written by two people.: The passage says authorship of some essays is disputed, not that they were jointly written. ✗
  • Choice D — The text describes why a collection of essays is notable and then details its publication history.: The second part of the passage is about authorship uncertainty, not publication history. ✗
That matches Choice A — The text mentions a collection of essays and then points out something about these essays that is not completely known..
